rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical [2+1] cycloaddition of electron-rich alkenes with diazo compounds to give cyclopropanes, and [2+2] cycloaddition with styrenes to give cyclobutanes, both via anodically generated alkene radical cations
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of an electron-rich alkene (e.g., trans-anethole) to an electrophilic alkene radical cation; cathodic reduction of the downstream cyclopropyl/cyclobutyl radical cation closes the cycle

key_intermediates.name: alkene radical cation of electron-rich styrene (e.g., anethole)
cyclopropyl radical cation (E) after N2 extrusion
cyclobutanyl radical cation (H) from radical cation + styrene

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes the electron-rich alkene A to radical cation B; B is attacked by the nucleophilic diazo compound C to give distonic radical cation D, which extrudes N2 to give cyclopropyl radical cation E, then cathodic reduction yields cyclopropane F. In the [2+2] manifold, B adds to a second styrene G to form cyclobutanyl radical cation H, which is reduced at the cathode (or via chain) to cyclobutane I.

probe_reagents.name: E/Z isomeric alkene substrates (stereochemical probe)
probe_reagents.observation: Both E and Z olefins converge to only trans-cyclopropanes, consistent with a stepwise radical-cation intermediate rather than a concerted pathway

probe_reagents.name: electricity on/off control
probe_reagents.observation: No product formed without applied current, confirming electrochemical initiation is essential

probe_reagents.name: tetrasubstituted/stilbene substrates with NO2
probe_reagents.observation: Electron-poor stilbene (NO2) still reactive (58%) because electrochemistry accesses oxidation potentials outside photoredox/Fe(III) window; tetrasubstituted olefin unreactive

notes: Authors explicitly note they cannot exclude a radical chain pathway in which radical cations E and H are reduced by an incoming neutral alkene (chain propagation) rather than at the cathode. Mechanism is proposed by analogy to prior Bauld/Ferreira/Kang radical-cation cycloadditions; no DFT, EPR, TEMPO trapping, or KIE experiments are reported.
